rawText:
For the second consecutive year, no country in the Anglosphere -- not America, Britain, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, nor Ireland -- has cracked the top ten of the World Happiness Report, even as Finland, a nation whose proverbs extol silence over speech, has clinched the title of the world's happiest country for the ninth time running, suggesting that something particular to the English-speaking world has gone profoundly wrong.

The divergence is starkest among the young, with under-25s across all six Anglophone nations recording some of the steepest declines in life satisfaction of any demographic group worldwide -- a slide that began in the early 2010s as smartphones and algorithm-driven social platforms proliferated, though the causal link remains frustratingly elusive given that Latin Americans, who are equally heavy users of social media, report surprisingly high levels of cheeriness.

What distinguishes the discontented Anglosphere from its happier counterparts may be less the technology itself than the cultural soil in which it has taken root -- societies that prize individualism and self-reliance, where economic precarity has deepened alongside rising costs, and where the gap between professed ideals of meritocracy and lived experience breeds a peculiarly corrosive form of disappointment.

Britain, which exemplifies this malaise, has found in the launch of "SNL UK" -- a live sketch comedy show imported from America -- both a symptom of its cultural exhaustion and a tentative remedy, with commissioners at Sky arguing that in dark times characterised by war, economic instability, and political polarisation, people need the release valve of laughter more than ever.

The inaugural episode's most resonant sketch portrayed the British prime minister as a feeble flip-flopper terrified of the man in the White House, a piece of satire that went viral precisely because it articulated a sense of national impotence that polls and happiness surveys struggle to quantify but that citizens recognise instinctively.

That comedy should emerge as a pressure valve for democratic discontent is not new, but the medium through which it circulates has changed utterly: posts related to sketch comedy generated 4.5 billion impressions on Reddit, YouTube, and X in a single month, indicating that the appetite for collective catharsis -- the shared experience of watching things go awry in real time -- has migrated online, where it can spread far beyond its country of origin.

Even the most intimate sphere of life -- romantic relationships -- bears the imprint of this cultural drift, for as one culture correspondent discovered while reading Ovid's two-thousand-year-old advice on the "art of loving," the practical tips on grooming and courtship feel uncannily contemporary, suggesting that while technology and norms have transformed dating into a landscape of apps and AI "lovers," the underlying human anxieties about attraction and rejection remain stubbornly unchanged.

The fact that ancient Roman dating counsel -- from dental hygiene to leg-bronzing -- reads like material from a modern lifestyle blog underscores a disquieting possibility: that much of what the Western world experiences as novel discontent is, in truth, a perennial human condition amplified by the unprecedented capacity of digital platforms to broadcast individual unhappiness into a collective narrative of decline.

Surveys from the region that defies the Anglosphere's gloom reveal a telling distinction between platforms built for communication, such as WhatsApp, which are associated with higher life satisfaction, and algorithm-driven feeds like TikTok and X, which curate content for engagement rather than connection -- yet Anglophone teenagers use a similar mix of both and still fare worse, hinting that the problem runs deeper than any particular app.

Whether this pervasive Western discontent will prove transient -- a generational adjustment to the dislocation wrought by rapid technological change -- or whether it marks a more fundamental fraying of the social contract that once bound citizens to shared institutions and purposes remains, like the happiness report itself, a question whose answer depends less on the data than on the political and cultural choices that lie ahead.

overview.title: Modern Discontent: Why the Western World Feels Adrift

sentences.original: For the second consecutive year, no country in the Anglosphere -- not America, Britain, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, nor Ireland -- has cracked the top ten of the World Happiness Report, even as Finland, a nation whose proverbs extol silence over speech, has clinched the title of the world's happiest country for the ninth time running, suggesting that something particular to the English-speaking world has gone profoundly wrong.
sentences.translation: 2년 연속으로 영어권 국가 중 어느 나라도 — 미국, 영국, 캐나다, 호주, 뉴질랜드, 아일랜드 그 어느 곳도 — 세계 행복 보고서 상위 10위 안에 들지 못했으며, 속담에서 말보다 침묵을 칭송하는 나라 핀란드가 9번째로 세계에서 가장 행복한 나라라는 타이틀을 거머쥔 것은 영어권 세계에 무언가 근본적으로 잘못된 것이 있음을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: For the second consecutive year
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사구

grammarElements.text: no country in the Anglosphere
grammarElements.label: 주어 (부정)

grammarElements.text: has cracked the top ten
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: even as Finland ... has clinched the title
grammarElements.label: even as 양보·대조절

grammarElements.text: suggesting that something ... has gone profoundly wrong
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: The team clinched the championship with a last-minute goal.
Finnish culture extols the virtues of quiet resilience.

expressions.usage: 순위 경쟁에서 상위권에 들었는지 논할 때 쓰는 비격식 표현

patterns.pattern: For [time period], no [group] has [achieved X], even as [contrasting group] has [achieved Y]
patterns.explanation: 한쪽의 실패와 다른 쪽의 성공을 대비시켜 문제를 부각하는 패턴
examples: For three years, no European bank has outperformed, even as Asian lenders have surged.
For a decade, no new entrant has broken through, even as incumbents have thrived.

drillQuestions.question: No country in the Anglosphere has ______ the top ten of the World Happiness Report.

sentences.notes: even as 대조절이 핀란드의 성공과 영어권의 실패를 한 문장에 병치시키며 글의 핵심 문제를 제기한다.

koreanAlignment.en: For the second consecutive year
has cracked the top ten
a nation whose proverbs extol silence over speech
something particular to the English-speaking world has gone profoundly wrong
koreanAlignment.ko: 영어권 세계에 무언가 근본적으로 잘못된 것이 있다

sentences.original: The divergence is starkest among the young, with under-25s across all six Anglophone nations recording some of the steepest declines in life satisfaction of any demographic group worldwide -- a slide that began in the early 2010s as smartphones and algorithm-driven social platforms proliferated, though the causal link remains frustratingly elusive given that Latin Americans, who are equally heavy users of social media, report surprisingly high levels of cheeriness.
sentences.translation: 이 격차는 젊은 층에서 가장 두드러지며, 6개 영어권 국가 모두에서 25세 미만 인구가 전 세계 어떤 인구 집단보다도 가파른 삶의 만족도 하락을 기록했다 — 스마트폰과 알고리즘 기반 소셜 플랫폼이 확산되기 시작한 2010년대 초에 시작된 하락이지만, 소셜 미디어를 똑같이 많이 사용하는 중남미인들이 놀라울 만큼 높은 행복감을 보고한다는 점에서 인과 관계는 답답할 만큼 규명하기 어렵다.

grammarElements.text: The divergence
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is starkest among the young
grammarElements.label: be동사 + 최상급 보어

grammarElements.text: with under-25s ... recording some of the steepest declines
grammarElements.label: with + 분사 구문 (부대상황)

grammarElements.text: though the causal link remains frustratingly elusive
grammarElements.label: though 양보절

vocabulary.example: A clear explanation for the trend remains elusive.

expressions.phrase: the causal link remains frustratingly elusive
expressions.meaning: 인과 관계가 답답할 만큼 규명하기 어렵다
expressions.usage: 상관관계가 있으나 인과를 증명할 수 없을 때 학술적·저널리즘적으로 쓰는 표현

patterns.explanation: 데이터를 제시한 뒤 양보절로 해석의 복잡성을 덧붙이는 패턴
examples: The decline is starkest among rural areas, with population falling 20%, though migration patterns complicate the picture.
The gap is starkest among women, with pay lagging behind, though education levels have risen.

drillQuestions.question: The causal link remains frustratingly ______ given that Latin Americans report high levels of cheeriness.

sentences.notes: though 절이 스마트폰 원인론의 한계를 제시하며 단순 인과가 아닌 복합적 분석을 유도한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The divergence is starkest among the young
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 격차는 젊은 층에서 가장 두드러진다

koreanAlignment.en: some of the steepest declines in life satisfaction
the causal link remains frustratingly elusive
koreanAlignment.ko: 인과 관계는 답답할 만큼 규명하기 어렵다

sentences.original: What distinguishes the discontented Anglosphere from its happier counterparts may be less the technology itself than the cultural soil in which it has taken root -- societies that prize individualism and self-reliance, where economic precarity has deepened alongside rising costs, and where the gap between professed ideals of meritocracy and lived experience breeds a peculiarly corrosive form of disappointment.
sentences.translation: 불만에 찬 영어권을 더 행복한 나라들과 구별 짓는 것은 기술 자체가 아니라, 기술이 뿌리내린 문화적 토양일지 모른다 — 개인주의와 자립을 중시하고, 비용 상승과 함께 경제적 불안정이 깊어졌으며, 표방하는 능력주의의 이상과 실제 삶의 경험 사이의 괴리가 유독 부식적인 실망감을 낳는 사회들 말이다.

grammarElements.text: What distinguishes the discontented Anglosphere from its happier counterparts
grammarElements.label: What 명사절 주어

grammarElements.text: may be less the technology itself than the cultural soil
grammarElements.label: less A than B 비교 보어

grammarElements.text: in which it has taken root
grammarElements.label: 전치사 + 관계대명사절

grammarElements.text: societies that prize individualism and self-reliance
grammarElements.label: 동격 설명구

vocabulary.example: Economic precarity has become a defining feature of young adult life.
Chronic inequality has a corrosive effect on social trust.

expressions.usage: 흔한 원인을 부정하고 더 깊은 원인을 제시할 때 사용하는 비교 구문

patterns.pattern: What distinguishes [group A] from [group B] may be less [obvious factor] than [deeper factor]
patterns.explanation: 표면적 원인을 넘어 구조적 원인을 제시하는 분석 패턴
examples: What distinguishes successful cities from struggling ones may be less geography than governance.
What distinguishes resilient economies from fragile ones may be less resources than institutions.

drillQuestions.question: The gap between professed ideals and lived experience breeds a peculiarly ______ form of disappointment.

sentences.notes: less A than B 구조로 기술이 아닌 문화적 토양이 핵심이라는 논점을 세운다.

koreanAlignment.en: the cultural soil in which it has taken root
societies that prize individualism and self-reliance
the gap between professed ideals of meritocracy and lived experience
koreanAlignment.ko: 표방하는 능력주의의 이상과 실제 삶의 경험 사이의 괴리

sentences.original: Britain, which exemplifies this malaise, has found in the launch of "SNL UK" -- a live sketch comedy show imported from America -- both a symptom of its cultural exhaustion and a tentative remedy, with commissioners at Sky arguing that in dark times characterised by war, economic instability, and political polarisation, people need the release valve of laughter more than ever.
sentences.translation: 이러한 권태를 가장 잘 보여주는 영국은, 미국에서 수입한 라이브 스케치 코미디 쇼 'SNL UK'의 출범에서 문화적 탈진의 증상이자 잠정적 처방을 동시에 발견했는데, 스카이 방송의 편성 책임자들은 전쟁과 경제 불안, 정치적 양극화로 특징지어지는 어두운 시대에 사람들이 그 어느 때보다 웃음이라는 안전밸브를 필요로 한다고 주장한다.

grammarElements.text: Britain, which exemplifies this malaise
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 비제한적 관계절

grammarElements.text: has found
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: both a symptom of its cultural exhaustion and a tentative remedy
grammarElements.label: both A and B 목적어

grammarElements.text: with commissioners at Sky arguing that ...
grammarElements.label: with + 분사 구문

vocabulary.example: A deep economic malaise has settled over the country.

expressions.usage: 억눌린 감정이나 압력이 빠져나가는 수단을 비유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: [Country], which exemplifies [problem], has found in [phenomenon] both [reading A] and [reading B]
patterns.explanation: 하나의 현상에 대한 이중적 해석을 동시에 제시하는 패턴
examples: Japan, which exemplifies demographic decline, has found in robotics both a coping mechanism and a cultural statement.
America, which exemplifies polarisation, has found in podcasts both a symptom of fragmentation and a tool for connection.

drillQuestions.question: Britain has found in the launch of "SNL UK" both a symptom and a tentative ______.

sentences.notes: both A and B 구조로 하나의 문화 현상에 대한 이중적 해석(증상이자 처방)을 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Britain, which exemplifies this malaise
koreanAlignment.ko: 이러한 권태를 가장 잘 보여주는 영국

koreanAlignment.en: both a symptom of its cultural exhaustion and a tentative remedy
the release valve of laughter

pronunciationNotes: malaise: /məˈleɪz/ 프랑스어 유래, 두 번째 음절 강세, '말레이즈'

sentences.original: The inaugural episode's most resonant sketch portrayed the British prime minister as a feeble flip-flopper terrified of the man in the White House, a piece of satire that went viral precisely because it articulated a sense of national impotence that polls and happiness surveys struggle to quantify but that citizens recognise instinctively.
sentences.translation: 첫 회의 가장 공명을 일으킨 스케치는 영국 총리를 백악관의 인물에게 겁먹은 나약한 우유부단한 사람으로 그렸는데, 이 풍자가 정확히 바이럴이 된 이유는 여론조사와 행복 설문이 수치화하기 힘들지만 시민들이 본능적으로 인식하는 국가적 무력감을 언어화했기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: The inaugural episode's most resonant sketch
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: portrayed
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: the British prime minister as a feeble flip-flopper
grammarElements.label: portray A as B 구조

grammarElements.text: a piece of satire that went viral precisely because it articulated ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 관계절 + because 이유절

vocabulary.example: The comedian articulated what millions had felt but could not express.

expressions.usage: 온라인 콘텐츠가 폭발적으로 확산되었을 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Content] went viral precisely because it articulated [unspoken feeling]
patterns.explanation: 콘텐츠의 성공 이유를 '표현되지 못한 감정의 언어화'로 설명하는 패턴
examples: The meme went viral precisely because it articulated widespread frustration with bureaucracy.
The speech went viral precisely because it articulated the anxiety that no politician had dared name.

drillQuestions.question: The satire went viral precisely because it ______ a sense of national impotence.

sentences.notes: portray A as B와 went viral because 구조가 결합되어 풍자의 사회적 기능을 분석한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The inaugural episode's most resonant sketch
koreanAlignment.ko: 첫 회의 가장 공명을 일으킨 스케치

koreanAlignment.en: a feeble flip-flopper
a sense of national impotence
citizens recognise instinctively

sentences.original: That comedy should emerge as a pressure valve for democratic discontent is not new, but the medium through which it circulates has changed utterly: posts related to sketch comedy generated 4.5 billion impressions on Reddit, YouTube, and X in a single month, indicating that the appetite for collective catharsis -- the shared experience of watching things go awry in real time -- has migrated online, where it can spread far beyond its country of origin.
sentences.translation: 코미디가 민주적 불만의 압력 해소 밸브로 부상하는 것 자체는 새로운 일이 아니지만, 그것이 유통되는 매체는 완전히 바뀌었다. 스케치 코미디 관련 게시물이 한 달 만에 레딧, 유튜브, X에서 45억 회의 노출을 기록했으며, 이는 집단적 카타르시스에 대한 갈망 — 실시간으로 일이 틀어지는 것을 함께 지켜보는 공유된 경험 — 이 온라인으로 이동하여 발원지를 훨씬 넘어 퍼질 수 있게 되었음을 나타낸다.

grammarElements.text: That comedy should emerge as a pressure valve
grammarElements.label: That 명사절 주어

grammarElements.text: is not new
grammarElements.label: be동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: but the medium through which it circulates has changed utterly
grammarElements.label: but 대조절

grammarElements.text: indicating that the appetite for collective catharsis has migrated online
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: Comedy provides a form of collective catharsis during stressful times.

expressions.usage: 계획이나 상황이 예상과 다르게 흘러갈 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: That [phenomenon] should [verb] is not new, but [aspect] has changed utterly
patterns.explanation: 현상 자체는 익숙하지만 그 양상이 근본적으로 달라졌음을 보여주는 패턴
examples: That politicians should lie is not new, but the speed at which lies spread has changed utterly.
That markets should crash is not new, but the interconnectedness that amplifies them has changed utterly.

drillQuestions.question: The appetite for collective catharsis has migrated ______, where it can spread far beyond its country of origin.

sentences.notes: That절 주어 구문이 격식적 논평 톤을 만들고, 콜론 뒤 데이터가 주장을 뒷받침한다.

koreanAlignment.en: That comedy should emerge as a pressure valve for democratic discontent
koreanAlignment.ko: 코미디가 민주적 불만의 압력 해소 밸브로 부상하는 것

koreanAlignment.en: the medium through which it circulates has changed utterly
the appetite for collective catharsis
has migrated online

pronunciationNotes: catharsis: /kəˈθɑːrsɪs/ 두 번째 음절에 강세, th는 /θ/ 무성음

sentences.original: Even the most intimate sphere of life -- romantic relationships -- bears the imprint of this cultural drift, for as one culture correspondent discovered while reading Ovid's two-thousand-year-old advice on the "art of loving," the practical tips on grooming and courtship feel uncannily contemporary, suggesting that while technology and norms have transformed dating into a landscape of apps and AI "lovers," the underlying human anxieties about attraction and rejection remain stubbornly unchanged.
sentences.translation: 삶의 가장 내밀한 영역인 연애조차 이 문화적 표류의 흔적을 지니고 있다. 한 문화 담당 기자가 오비디우스의 2천 년 된 '사랑의 기술' 조언을 읽으면서 발견한 바에 따르면, 외모 관리와 구애에 대한 실용적 조언이 섬뜩할 만큼 현대적이었으며, 이는 기술과 규범이 연애를 앱과 AI '연인'의 세계로 바꾸었음에도 매력과 거절에 대한 근본적 인간 불안은 완고하게 변하지 않았음을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: Even the most intimate sphere of life
grammarElements.label: 주어 (Even 강조)

grammarElements.text: bears the imprint of this cultural drift
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: for as one culture correspondent discovered
grammarElements.label: for 이유절 + as 시간절

grammarElements.text: suggesting that ... the underlying human anxieties ... remain stubbornly unchanged
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결론)

vocabulary.example: The ancient text felt uncannily relevant to modern readers.

expressions.usage: 어떤 현상이 더 큰 경향의 영향을 받았음을 표현하는 격식적 비유

patterns.pattern: Even [most intimate domain] bears the imprint of [broader trend]
patterns.explanation: 거대한 사회적 흐름이 가장 사적인 영역까지 침투했음을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Even family dinners bear the imprint of the attention economy.
Even friendships bear the imprint of social media's performative culture.

drillQuestions.question: The underlying human anxieties about attraction and rejection remain stubbornly ______.

sentences.notes: Even으로 시작해 연애라는 사적 영역까지 사회적 불만이 침투했음을 보여주는 확대 기법이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Even the most intimate sphere of life
bears the imprint of this cultural drift
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 문화적 표류의 흔적을 지니고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: the practical tips feel uncannily contemporary
remain stubbornly unchanged

sentences.original: The fact that ancient Roman dating counsel -- from dental hygiene to leg-bronzing -- reads like material from a modern lifestyle blog underscores a disquieting possibility: that much of what the Western world experiences as novel discontent is, in truth, a perennial human condition amplified by the unprecedented capacity of digital platforms to broadcast individual unhappiness into a collective narrative of decline.
sentences.translation: 고대 로마의 연애 조언이 — 치아 위생에서 다리 태닝에 이르기까지 — 현대 라이프스타일 블로그의 글처럼 읽힌다는 사실은 불안한 가능성을 부각한다: 서구 세계가 새로운 불만으로 경험하는 것의 상당 부분은 실상 개인의 불행을 집단적 쇠퇴 서사로 방송하는 디지털 플랫폼의 전례 없는 능력에 의해 증폭된 영원한 인간 조건이라는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: The fact that ancient Roman dating counsel reads like material from a modern lifestyle blog
grammarElements.label: The fact that 명사절 주어

grammarElements.text: underscores
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: a disquieting possibility
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: that much of what the Western world experiences as novel discontent is ... a perennial human condition
grammarElements.label: 동격 that절 (possibility의 내용)

vocabulary.example: Loneliness is a perennial human concern, not a modern invention.

expressions.usage: 증거가 불편한 결론을 뒷받침함을 격식적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: The fact that [surprising observation] underscores [unsettling conclusion]
patterns.explanation: 놀라운 사실을 제시한 뒤 그것이 가리키는 불편한 진실로 연결하는 패턴
examples: The fact that algorithms can predict divorce better than therapists underscores how patterned human behaviour really is.
The fact that AI can write poetry underscores how formulaic much creative writing has become.

drillQuestions.question: Much of what the Western world experiences as novel discontent is, in truth, a ______ human condition.

sentences.notes: 콜론으로 핵심 논점을 전달하며, '새로운 불만'이 실은 '오래된 조건의 증폭'이라는 반전을 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: ancient Roman dating counsel
underscores a disquieting possibility
a perennial human condition
broadcast individual unhappiness into a collective narrative of decline
koreanAlignment.ko: 개인의 불행을 집단적 쇠퇴 서사로 방송하다

sentences.original: Surveys from the region that defies the Anglosphere's gloom reveal a telling distinction between platforms built for communication, such as WhatsApp, which are associated with higher life satisfaction, and algorithm-driven feeds like TikTok and X, which curate content for engagement rather than connection -- yet Anglophone teenagers use a similar mix of both and still fare worse, hinting that the problem runs deeper than any particular app.
sentences.translation: 영어권의 우울에 저항하는 지역의 설문조사는 의미심장한 구분을 드러낸다: 왓츠앱처럼 소통을 위해 만들어져 더 높은 삶의 만족도와 연관된 플랫폼과, 틱톡이나 X처럼 연결이 아니라 참여를 위해 콘텐츠를 큐레이션하는 알고리즘 기반 피드 사이의 구분이다 — 그러나 영어권 청소년들이 이 두 가지를 비슷하게 혼합 사용하면서도 여전히 더 나쁜 결과를 보이는 것은 문제가 특정 앱보다 더 깊은 곳에 있음을 암시한다.

grammarElements.text: Surveys from the region that defies the Anglosphere's gloom
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: reveal
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: a telling distinction between ... and ...
grammarElements.label: between A and B 목적어

grammarElements.text: yet Anglophone teenagers use a similar mix ... and still fare worse
grammarElements.label: yet 대조절

vocabulary.example: Algorithms curate content based on what keeps users scrolling.

expressions.usage: 표면적 원인 너머의 구조적 문제가 있음을 시사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Data] reveal a telling distinction between [A] and [B] -- yet [complication]
patterns.explanation: 데이터로 구분을 제시한 뒤 yet 절로 그 구분만으로는 부족함을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Studies reveal a telling distinction between exercise types -- yet patients who follow both still report mixed results.
Experiments reveal a telling distinction between teaching methods -- yet schools that combine them see no improvement.

drillQuestions.question: Anglophone teenagers use a similar mix of both and still fare ______, hinting that the problem runs deeper.

sentences.notes: yet 절이 앞서 제시한 플랫폼 유형 구분의 설명력 한계를 드러내며 더 깊은 원인을 암시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: a telling distinction
platforms built for communication
curate content for engagement rather than connection
koreanAlignment.ko: 연결이 아니라 참여를 위해 콘텐츠를 큐레이션하다

koreanAlignment.en: the problem runs deeper than any particular app
koreanAlignment.ko: 문제가 특정 앱보다 더 깊은 곳에 있다

sentences.original: Whether this pervasive Western discontent will prove transient -- a generational adjustment to the dislocation wrought by rapid technological change -- or whether it marks a more fundamental fraying of the social contract that once bound citizens to shared institutions and purposes remains, like the happiness report itself, a question whose answer depends less on the data than on the political and cultural choices that lie ahead.
sentences.translation: 이 만연한 서구의 불만이 일시적인 것으로 드러날지 — 급속한 기술 변화가 초래한 혼란에 대한 세대적 적응일 뿐인지 — 아니면 시민을 공유된 제도와 목적에 묶어 두던 사회계약의 더 근본적인 해체를 의미하는지는, 행복 보고서 자체가 그러하듯, 데이터보다는 앞으로의 정치적·문화적 선택에 달린 질문으로 남아 있다.

grammarElements.text: Whether this pervasive Western discontent will prove transient
grammarElements.label: Whether A or B 명사절 주어

grammarElements.text: or whether it marks a more fundamental fraying of the social contract
grammarElements.label: or whether 병렬절

grammarElements.text: remains
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: a question whose answer depends less on the data than on the political and cultural choices
grammarElements.label: 보어 + less A than B 비교

vocabulary.example: Some analysts believe the slowdown is transient rather than structural.
The fraying of social trust makes collective action harder.

expressions.usage: 시민과 국가 혹은 시민 상호 간의 묵시적 합의를 가리키는 정치철학 용어

patterns.pattern: Whether [outcome A] or whether [outcome B] remains a question whose answer depends on [factor]
patterns.explanation: 열린 결말로 마무리하며 독자에게 판단을 넘기는 결론 패턴
examples: Whether the rally is sustainable or whether it masks deeper weakness remains a question whose answer depends on earnings.
Whether AI enhances creativity or diminishes it remains a question whose answer depends on how we choose to use it.

drillQuestions.question: Whether this discontent will prove transient or marks a more fundamental ______ of the social contract remains to be seen.

sentences.notes: Whether A or whether B 구조로 글을 열린 질문으로 마무리하며, less A than B가 데이터보다 선택이 중요함을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: this pervasive Western discontent
a generational adjustment to the dislocation wrought by rapid technological change
koreanAlignment.ko: 급속한 기술 변화가 초래한 혼란에 대한 세대적 적응

koreanAlignment.en: a more fundamental fraying of the social contract
the political and cultural choices that lie ahead
